desc:
There is a heavy darkness here in this dell, deep within the forest's clutches,
that has been hidden from the light of the sun for ages unknown. Twisted
branches and tree trunks are densely intertwined and overhead a dense canopy
obscures all light. Underfoot, snarling roots and fallen debris lie as snares to
trip the unwary.
name: Under a Dark Canopy

desc:
Hawthorn bushes may be found beneath the shaded canopy of this area of oak.
Elder also grow beneath the mighty oaks, rising to nearly ten yards at their
best, wherever some slight gap in the canopy allows light to reach the forest
floor. In particular, the elder and hawthorn have flourished where the forest is
cut through by a river, running its way through the western and northern fringes
of this land.
name: A Shaded Arena

desc:
The water sparkles as light from above pierces the forest canopy. Perfectly
rounded pebbles can be seen distinctly through the deep, clear water. A complex
variety of woodland loving flowers have made their homes alongside the grassy
riverbanks to either side, whilst overhead you can see the branches of trees
that extend out over the river.
name: A Sparkling River

desc:
Beautiful full-grown trees overlook a sparkling river to the south. A great
variety of trees have made this land their home, whilst lush verdant grass tries
to make its presence known on the forest floor. Mosses and lichen cover the
trunks of the trees and several trees have branches extending over the river.
name: On a Wooded River Bank

desc:
A chill wind drifts through the forest here and runs straight to the bones. A
very thick and heavy mist covers the ground, making it nearly impossible not to
trip over whatever may lie there. Very little light makes it through the thick
mass of branches and leaves that hang overhead.
name: A Cold, Gloomy Forest

desc:
With the golden prairies still visible to the north and west, this part of the
forest is very inviting. Light still filters in from the north leaving this part
of the forest edge well lit. To the south, the forest darkens as the light has
to filter through the thick canopy of the forest.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
The lush deep green of the trees to the south showers the golden grass in
colourful arrays, giving the area a surreal appearance. The depth of the forest
eerily call to the traveller to enter and explore the heart of the forest.
However, the stillness of the air and the soundless surroundings would make any
adventurer wary to travel in that forest.
name: Approach to the Forest

desc:
The marsh here is covered in all sizes and sorts of reeds as the water pushes
its way over the side of the river and into the shallow lowlands. The swift
current can be heard to the north as the water makes its trek to the great river
in the east. The odd sound of some insects is the only thing that breaks the
monotony of the sound of water.
name: Rustling Reeds

desc:
A number of small pools have formed here off from the main flow of the river.
This seems like a perfect breeding place for fish and the perfect hunting place
for predators. However, the only wildlife that seems to be living here are a few
fish that can be seen in the pools. The river flows out of the north and
continues to the east. Small marshes seem to have formed in the other
directions.
name: A Shallow River

desc:
The river here flows from the west to the south, and the current picks up
slightly as it hits some very small and tame rapids. The north bank is raised
too much to allow anyone to climb up out of the river. To the west, the Misty
Mountains glare down from afar.
name: A Shallow River

desc:
This tiny meadow seems completely cut off from the rest of the world. At one
time, this meadow appears to have been a sprawling garden. Remnants of flowers
that were once well-tended are all around. To the south and west, you can hear a
river as it continues on its everlasting journey, although a stand of bushes and
trees blocks your way in those directions. The only way out seems to be into the
foothills to the north.
name: A Secluded Meadow

desc:
The foothills here are barren of everything. Not even grass seems to grow here.
It is as though the earth here was scarred long ago and has never fully
recovered. A small meadow lies to the south, and beyond that, a river flows
south. To the north, a small statue is visible on top of one of the hills, and
more hills spring up everywhere else. From the tops of the hills, the Misty
Mountains seem to cover the entire western horizon.
name: Barren Hills

desc:
On the top of one of the hills, there is a large stone, standing straight up as
if guarding something. The foothills continue in all directions, and the sound
of running water comes faintly from the south and possibly from the distant east
as well. In the distance, the Misty Mountains are vague shapes on the western
horizon.
name: A Large Stone on a Hill

desc:
The lands all around seem so inviting, but there is something about these hills
that must emanate a less than comfortable aura as none have dared set up a
homestead or even travelled very often through this land. Far off to the west
the peaks of the Misty Mountains are barely visible.
name: Foothills

desc:
A few small rocks and stones intersperse the soil here. Some larger boulders are
also strewn across the fields, pressing down even the last bit of healthy grass.
The surface of dirt is hard-packed and riddled with cracks from the lack of
moisture, giving this area a barren and abandoned look. The dusty and rocky
plains continue towards all directions, and sometimes, animal life can hardly be
noticed.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
This dirt-trodden area is bare and barren beside a few stunted and sick-looking
trees which have managed to take root here. The grass is of yellowish colour
with thin blades and only grows in small scattered patches. A few shrubs and
spiny plants have also managed to survive despite the poor conditions.
name: Dusty Plains

desc:
Bare earth and tufts of sick grass share the open space here, giving these
fields a burnt look. The ground is grey, dusty, and devoid of plants or foliage,
apart from a small amount of crabgrass or a single spiny shrub here or there. A
fallen tree lies on the ground, rotten and hollow inside. Its stump has already
begun to decay.
name: Sickened Fields

desc:
The stench of sun-baked dirt spreads over the surrounding plains. Dust covers
everything with a thin layer of light brown. Some large bushes grow in the
rock-pebbled dirt, their branches supporting only a few leaves. The rest have
fallen down, lying under the bushes in a state of decay. A few weeds have taken
root, and a few tufts of fresh grass grow on the ground, fighting for survival.
name: Acrid Fields

desc:
A thin layer of dust covers everything here. The grass has a slight brown tinge
because of the dirt and dust that covers it. The few bushes that inhabit these
fields are thin and spiny, apparently having lost their leaves due to the lack
of nutritious soil. A few brownish plants grow in between the grass. To the
south, the grass seems to be fading out of the plains, leaving only bare earth
underfoot.
name: Dust-covered Plains

desc:
The trail that comes from the north narrows at this point. Small bushes and
foliage line the walkway, forcing travellers to pass this area in single file.
The surrounding fields are full of healthy and verdant grass, swaying in the
passing breezes. A few large trees grow at the edge of this path, occasionally
offering shadow.
name: Bush-lined Trail

desc:
A selection of tufted and large flowering dark green grasses loosely carpet the
open beech wood floor. The trees form a canopy, providing a fair amount of shade
beneath their branches. Patches of the sky may be seen peeking through the
forest ceiling.
name: Beech Woodland

desc:
The trunks of the pine trees, predominant in this area, are cast into constant
shadow by the thick, green canopy. Layers of thin branches stand out at right
angles to the trunks, whilst needle-like leaves cover both the branches and the
forest floor beneath you. The surrounding air is perfumed with an ageless scent
of pine. A steady rumbling sound can often be heard from the north.
name: Amongst Tall Pines

desc:
This area of the forest consists of a number of stunted pine trees on the rough
craggy slopes of an outlying portion of the mountains, barely visible far to the
west. Here some height allows one to look out over the vast expanse of forest,
which stretches off into the horizon as far as the eyes can see. A steady rumble
of thunder emanates from the south as a waterfall plummets from a fast moving
river.
name: Amongst Stunted Pine Trees

desc:
This part of the forest is slightly sunken compared to the rest of the area.
However, the decline in altitude has not affected the growth of the trees, and
in fact, many tall and ancient oak trees cover the land. The heavy mist lays
thick around everything, as can be expected when the entire forest is covered in
it. The ways north and west are completely blocked by a sharp incline in the
land. The ways east and south have a much more gradual incline.
name: A Hollow in the Forest

desc:
The forest here mainly consists of large oak and beautiful white birch trees. A
thick layer of mist covers the forest floor, and from time to time, a green glow
from the light of the sun or moon filters through the canopy of the trees,
bringing a dim light to the area. Thick tree roots cover the forest floor, and
somehow some small bushes have managed to grow in the area. A wall of these
bushes blocks the way west, while in all other directions, the forest continues.
name: Dimly Lit Forest

desc:
The forest is quite dark here, especially considering the proximity to the open
plains to the north. A large black rock is the only marker in the sea of grass
in that direction. Covering the forest floor is a tangle of roots from the
various types of trees that grow in this part of the forest, and the fog that
always lies upon Fangorn Forest is not as pervasive here. The forest continues
to the south and east.
name: Inside the Border of the Forest

desc:
The golden grass here rushes to meet the green borders of the forest just to the
south. All seems to be relatively quiet in the expanse of prairie here, however,
a quiet humming sound can be faintly heard from the south. To the east, an arm
of the forest extends out into the prairie, and to the north, a huge black rock
sits in the middle of the grass.
name: Prairie before the Forest

desc:
Surrounded by tall prairie grass, with a stretch of forest to the east, this
large rock seems completely out of place. No moss attempts to cover its ancient
black surface. Almost thirty feet in diameter, the rock is impossible to move,
and seems to be made of a rare and unusual black stone.
name: A Huge Rock

desc:
At this point, the prairies seem to be fighting their way towards the sun, their
source of life. The rise onto which they climb seems to be some ancient remnants
of yet another arm of the great Misty Mountains that was cast down ages ago. To
the south, a large dark shape can be made out, lying flat on the ground.
name: A Small Rise in the Prairie

desc:
The golden grass here has risen onto a small rise in defiance of its
counterparts covering the eastern landscape. To the south, the rise continues,
while to the north, a small valley houses a grove of trees. The shadow of the
Misty Mountains seems to have avoided this place.
name: A Small Rise in the Prairie

desc:
Just below the foothills, this small grove stands in a shallow valley. Signs of
life can barely be seen here, as a small bird skirts from one tree to another.
This might be a great place to stop and rest before continuing through the
prairies and foothills that rise all around.
name: A Small Grove

desc:
The foothills seem to be alive with grass. The sounds of grazing animals and
birds can be heard from time to time. In the shadow of the great Misty Mountains
the lands are devoid of civilization, making this place a haven for wildlife.
name: Foothills near the Mountains

desc:
All around, the fields seem very inviting and warm to the soul. The fair green
grass looks as if it had been spared the trampling of feet and hoofs. To the
south, there are foothills continuing to a small grove of trees. The land to the
east seems to run on forever as the grass there takes on a golden colour.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
Grass-covered fields stretch out as far as the eye can see. Some bushes also
grow in small groves around the plains. Flowers of countless colours give the
fields a contrast to the bright green grass. To the south, another set of
foothills begins to slope upwards leading to the mountains further away in that
direction.
name: Flowery Grasslands

desc:
Vibrant grass grows all around on this field in varying shades of green. A few
clumped-up bush formations break the monotony of the knee-high grass. Some trees
stand in the middle of the bushes, looking like statues of some sort. The
greenery seems to continue as a far as the eye can see, like a flowing carpet of
grass.
name: Bushes in the Grass

desc:
Dark green grass covers these bushy fields. Below, their healthy roots grow in
the rich soil. A variety of shrubs and bushes grow in scattered clumps and its
own formations, berry plants being the most prominent kind of all. Tiny insects
seem to be feeding on the surrounding flora, and their constant buzz often
drifts on passing winds.
name: Grass-filled Bushland

desc:
Broad blades of verdant grass cover these fields. Small bushes of sun-dried
green and brown hues contrast the brighter colour of the grass. Small groves of
younger trees grow here and there on the grassland, offering shelter and a
resting place for passing travellers. A few rocky boulders lie scattered over
the grass, having been parted from the mountains to the north.
name: Verdant Bushy Plains

desc:
Protruding rocks and pebbles dominate this high slope that leads northwards
towards the mountain peak above. The hills are bare and covered with dirt and
flint, offering a small chance for plants to root. A few stunted trees have
managed to push their roots into the rocky surface, feeding on the remaining
soil in the cracks of the stone. Crabgrass and some other shrubs grow here and
there, shivering in passing winds. To the south, the barren landscape seems to
give way to healthy grassland.
name: Rocky Hills near Grasslands

desc:
This is the highest point of the surrounding mountain range. Pebbles and rock
flint cover the heavily sloping cliff, making it hard to traverse. A quick
glance southwards over the surrounding lands gives an excellent view over the
bush-filled grassland and small coppices, which dot the calm landscape.
name: On the Mountain Peak

desc:
A multitude of dimly-lit, wildly-interspersed nettles, out of control woodland
shrubs and hanging lichens crowd around, reaching out with snarls and creepers
for exposed flesh. The dark oak canopy overhead shields the glade from any
direct illumination, leaving the woodland in a perpetual state of dusk, dimly
lit by an eerie florescent glow.
name: An Eerie Glade

desc:
The break in the green canopy overhead reminds you of the world beyond the
forest's embrace. To keep the open sky above you it would be necessary to follow
the river north as it flows still generally eastwards in its own time. To the
west, a small, natural ford has formed in the river.
name: A Meander in the River's Course

desc:
Oak, ash, maple, and many other species have made this land their own.
Approaching the river, the plant life changes slightly. Not many trees are seen
along the riverside. Instead, shrubs, bushes and a variety of flowers can be
seen thriving there.
name: A Forest Approaching the River Limlight

desc:
Over time the direction of the river has slowly changed, cutting away at the
south bank as the river bends from the south to the east. The trees here grow at
a slight distance from the riverbanks and small shrubs and flowers have taken
root in the fertile land that lies beside the river. The river here steadily
deepen and the water remains clear.
name: The River Limlight

desc:
Common oak and sweet chestnut trees are densely interspersed throughout this
part of the forest. The ground beneath the trees is rich with tall grasses and
various woodland flowers, whilst occasionally a prickly chestnut case from some
long fallen seed of the chestnut trees may be kicked up from underfoot.
name: A Riverside Forest

desc:
A grove of maples stands here as a part of, yet apart from, the greater forest
landscape. Beneath their supple branches, a verdant meadow of soft grasses and
woodland flowers spreads along the riverbanks. The area has a look of a wild yet
cared for woodland, once well tended. Nowhere can be found evidence of who would
be responsible for such an endeavour.
name: Forest North of a Ford

desc:
The river here looks to be fordable, as little as two to three feet in depth at
its shallowest and no greater than five to six feet at its worst. The waters are
slow moving and clear, with a pebbled and gravelled base visible in the
shallowest parts. Signs of pond-weeds and other aquatic vegetation may be seen
here and there and the riverbanks are low enough to allow entrance and exit.
name: A Ford on River Limlight

desc:
A densely packed forest of oak and ash is interspersed with the occasional maple
tree, especially along the banks of the quietly flowing river to the north. The
banks of the watercourse at this point are low and stable, affording easy access
to the water.
name: Forest South of a Ford

desc:
Hummocks of wild fescue grasses interspersed with primroses, clovers and the
occasional orchid have made a home here on the banks of the stately flowing
waters that pass by to the north. Elder and willow grow here in a peaceful
harmony, drinking deeply from the heady waters of the Limlight and standing tall
and strong, free from disease or damage of any kind. The forest here seems less
musty and aged then elsewhere.
name: On the South Bank of a River

desc:
The river, while looking as if it at one time was quite deep, now seems to be
shallow and moving slow enough to be fordable. Perhaps because the riverbed is
wider here than to the south or east. The banks seem to be stable and are
covered in grasses, wild flowers and the more light-loving tree species. Here at
least, the generally stuffy, enclosed feeling of the forest is lessened
somewhat.
name: At a Widening of the Watercourse

desc:
Yellow primroses peek forth from beneath the tangled undergrowth, beneath a
canopy of elder, dogwood and ash woodland. This forest glade seems less stuffy
and dim than the vast, surrounding woodland. A fast flowing river bends around
the southern and western fringes of this area, widening and losing speed as it
turns.
name: Under Dogwood

desc:
A huge mass of woody, yellow-green plants grows high up on the branches of the
oak and poplar trees that call this area home. Fleshy white berries identify
these huge parasitic clumps as the evergreen plant known as mistletoe, although
it is rare indeed to see such a proliferation of this plant.
name: Beneath the Mistletoe

desc:
Filamentous growths of fungi encircle the exposed roots of the trees throughout
this area. The woodland primarily consists of birch and aspen, although some
hornbeam may also be seen here. The soil appears to be rich in organic matter
and despite the obvious fungi, the trees here all appear to healthy.
name: Filaments of Fungi

desc:
The fast flowing river cuts a steep bank into the eastern edge of the bend as
the river turns from an easterly to a southerly course. The inner curve of the
riverbank is gentle, allowing easy access to the river water while the outer
curve is populated by wet, mossy and slippery looking rocks and boulders.
name: At a Bend in the River

desc:
The lustrous greens and teals of the willow trees overhang the meandering banks
of the river as it turns from the north-west to the east. The crooked and
grey-green trunks of the crack willow wildly sprout their offshoots in all
directions, whilst the yellow-brown trunk and branches of the weeping willow
cascades downwards to drink deeply from the river's edge.
name: Overhanging Willow

desc:
The dominant trees of this overgrown glade have long straight trunks, with thick
branches rising from their upper reaches. The crowns of these trees are whorled
and they rise in height to over sixty feet. The bark of these trees is leathery
to touch, silvery grey or black and is cracked in many places. The branches show
a covering of dark wrinkled leaves, yet there is no sign that these trees have
ever fruited.
name: A Grove of Wild Cherry

desc:
The forest floor beneath this intermingled stand of deciduous woodland is
covered by a dancing mist of green and blue. A horde of bluebells have crept
forth from beneath the rustling leaf litter. The trees and other plant life here
seem to pay no heed to the seasons outside. To the north, the reflective sheen
of a watercourse may be seen through the tree line.
name: A Carpet of Bluebells

desc:
As the river's course shifts from the north back to the east in a grand sweeping
curve, it starts to slow, becoming more stately in its progress. The dense,
forested river banks to either side drop in height as the river slowly widens.
name: A Sweeping Bend in the River

desc:
The fast flowing waters of the River Limlight move through the forested
landscape from the mountains far to the west. The river winds its way through
Fangorns ageless grasp before passing into the east, joining the Anduin far
away. At this point in the rivers life, the waters are fresh and young, cold and
hard from their birth in the mountains, rich and ageless from the influence of
this most ancient woodland.
name: The River Limlight

desc:
Densely clustered colonies of a red, coral-like fungus cover many of the
recently fallen twigs, trunks and branches that lie upon the forest floor here,
slowly decomposing. A number of saplings and some semi-mature alder are growing
here between the occasional mature oak and ash. The woods appear to have been
thinned out at some time in the recent past, whether by some great storm or
through another agent is impossible to say.
name: A Fungal Bloom

desc:
The trees in this area of the forest have grown in a twisted tangle of jutting
roots, briars and branches. Vines and brambles choke new growth, whilst twisted
ash compete with broad and needle-leaved incursors. The heaviest congestion is
to the north, where the mass of vegetation has grown so great that no travel is
easily done in that direction. A fast flowing river can be seen and heard
flowing to the south.
name: Tangled Growth

desc:
A steep and swift moving watercourse, broken up by rapids and mini-waterfalls
passes through the ancient forest. Only few plants grow in this fast flowing
river, or on its steeply banked edges to either side. Although in several places
the roots of young and old trees are exposed where they jut out from the river
banks.
name: Rapids on the Limlight

desc:
The forest floor in this area of beech and lime woodland is interspersed with a
great number of jutting rocks, some of quite prominent height and girth. Huge
swathes of honeysuckle may be seen covering much of the scrub, rocks and the
lower branches of trees as it trails and scrambles its way through the forest.
name: Creeping Scrub

desc:
A claustrophobic tangle of honeysuckle, vines and creepers battle overhead for
domination as they weave between the branches of beech and ash. Underfoot, a
tangled mat of creeping vines, twisted roots and splintered rocky outcroppings
conspire to trip and turn you at every step.
name: A Densely Interwoven Tangle

desc:
An area of broad-leafed deciduous woodland extends in every direction. The
undergrowth beneath the trees cover is primarily composed of fallen leaf litter,
decaying extremely slowly in this dry unchanging land. Various brackens and
ferns sprout from around the base of the trees, providing some protection for
whatever may choose to live here.
name: Bracken and Ferns

desc:
Tangling vines and trailing creepers hang from the reaching branches of the oak,
beech and lime that populate this area. The tree canopy overhead is interwoven
with ivy and twisted creeping vines existing in fierce competition, with the
trees.
name: Twisted Creepers

desc:
Lichen cover the twigs and branches of many of the oak and ash giants that fill
the land. This area has a distinctly ancient feeling about it. The dusty greens
and greys of the trailing lichen that adorn many old trees here give them a
bearded appearance. A fast flowing river can be heard to the north.
name: Bearded Oaks

desc:
The canopy here reaches heights of thirty to forty yards and consists of trees
with crowns high on their trunks. The bark of the trees is orange-brown in
colour. Where some have previously been damaged by bad weather, a sticky resin
has been exuded. Sounds of a fast flowing river can be heard from the north, and
the trees grow right up to the edge of the steep riverbanks.
name: Coniferous Trees

desc:
An ancient oak tree, well over two yards in girth lies here, spanning the two
banks of the River Limlight, whilst the river itself flows furiously some three
yards below. The fallen tree seems to have lain here for some great length of
time and appears to have lost a fair amount of its bulk over the years to rot
and the river below.
name: A Fallen Tree Bridges the River

desc:
Throughout this area a large number of especially ancient trees stand tall
amongst lesser younger saplings. Reddish bark and dark leaves help to
distinguish these ancient trees as yew, the preferred wood of the master bowyer
and fletcher.
name: Ancient Woodland

desc:
This area of the forest comprises of a large number of coniferous trees, the
needles and cones of which have accumulated over many years into a deep litter
underfoot. Here and there, an occasional yew sapling has invaded from the east,
although these invaders are not doing well in the soil conditions here. A narrow
river can be seen flowing to the south.
name: Pine Trees, Needles and Cones

desc:
A cold wind blows up from the south through all the trees, and the faint sound
of water can be heard to the distant south, presumably the source of the cooling
wind. Tall evergreens mix with great white birch trees and a thin mist covers
the ground but does not quite obscure it completely. The canopy of the forest is
thin here, and much light breaks through the leaves. The prairies to the north
are visible and the forest continues in all other directions.
name: A Cool Forest

desc:
As the forest approaches the mountains in the far west, the number of fir trees
seems to be constantly growing and all other types of trees seem to dwindle.
Still, there are a number of oaks and birch trees with the odd tree being of a
completely different variety. The only light that can filter through the
treetops here is that of the sun, but even that can barely burrow its way
through the thick canopy of the forest.
name: A Forest of Firs

desc:
Tall, thin fir trees make up the majority of this part of the forest, with the
thick trunks of some oak trees providing a nice contrast. Some white birch trees
are intermingled between the other two species as well. The ever-present dim
green light filters through the treetops giving off some light to the area, but
barely enough to see by. A very ancient oak tree stands here, rising above the
other trees surrounding it. A massive trunk almost ten-feet wide makes this tree
especially astounding to see. The forest continues in all directions.
name: An Ancient Oak

desc:
The forest here is extremely dark. Barely a glimmer of light filters through the
treetops, and even that does not touch the ground. A heavy mist has set itself
upon the ground, obscuring anything that might lie below its thick vapours. The
only trees to be found in this area are large oaks. The forest brightens as it
progresses northwards and to the east. To the south and west it is nearly as
dark as here.
name: A Dark, Damp Forest

desc:
The gloom of the forest is overwhelming. An eerie mist surrounds most of the
ground, covering any undergrowth that may trip an unwary traveller. Large, regal
birch trees control this area of the forest, with a mix of fir and oak trees as
well. A very faint green glow penetrates the canopy of the forest, giving off
what little light there is. The forest continues in all directions.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
A light mist arises from the ground here, completely covering the forest floor.
The trees are closer together than in most of the surrounding forest. Dim green
light filters through the canopy of the forest making the mist glow green as it
shifts below your feet and around the trunks of the trees. Faint outlines of
uncovered roots are barely visible through the mist.
name: A Damp Forest

desc:
Darkness completely covers this part of the forest, the canopy is so thick that
not even the brightest sunlight can penetrate it. An eerie mist floats along the
ground, covering the forest floor. Even the trees themselves are dark, some
appearing a solid black in colour, yet the eyes can easily be mistaken in this
lighting.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
Set on the border of the forest, the trees here seem to have an almost ancient
quality. The occasional soft sound, perhaps a whisper on the wind, might cause
some to wonder if these trees are more aware than others one might find across
the lands. The forest border grows thinner to the north where the ground is
covered with vast plains.
name: Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
The forest borders are well lit, and the golden grass to the north and west is
visible through the light underbrush and trees. To the south, the forest quickly
darkens and only a dim green light echoes through the leaves, creating an eerie
glow to the forest. The east remains well lit.
name: Prairie near the Forest

desc:
To the south and to the east, the great Fangorn Forest looms in a splendid
display of nature at its prime. However, here the prairies still rule the
landscape, covering miles to the north in a rich golden colour. Long stalks
gently brush together creating a low rustling sound. Just to the west however,
the forest that once covered that area is now nothing more than rotting tree
stumps.
name: Prairie near a Forest

desc:
The landscape here is very dismal, covered in burnt grass and foliage as well as
being littered with tree stumps. The entire area smells of ash and decaying
wildlife. Many of the tree stumps are covered in thick green moss and were very
old judging by the size of them. The damage continues to the east and north.
name: Burnt Landscape

desc:
The way here is dimly lit as very little light filters through the leaves of the
trees, giving the area an eerie green luminescence. The trees here seem to form
a natural north-south path, which ends abruptly to the north as the forest
closes the path. The way to the east is completely blocked off by black scorched
trees.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
Darkness engulfs this part of the forest, and the mist that lies over the ground
is extremely dense, so as to completely cover the forest floor and whatever may
lie there. The trees here are very old, some can even be called ancient amongst
trees. Through the darkness, it is nearly impossible to make out exactly what
types of trees grow here. To the east, the forest brightens.
name: A Dark Forest

desc:
The forest thickens here, and becomes very dark. Only a very faint green glow
emanates through the treetops. A tall and very ancient evergreen grows here
among the birches and oaks, its age alone gives it a regal appearance, with
branches as thick as a troll's torso. By far, this must be one of the older
trees in the forest. The forest continues in all directions except to the north.
name: A Dark Forest

desc:
The forest here seems to shift around the wary traveller, but in this lighting,
the eyes can play tricks on the mind. A light mist covers the ground, obscuring
whatever lies beneath it. Coupled with a chill wind from the southeast, this
place feels as though it may be haunted. No sounds can be heard except for the
leaves rustling against each other. The forest darkens in all directions.
name: A Dimly Lit Forest

desc:
Tall firs and ancient oak trees populate much of this area of the forest.
Because of this, light still breaks through the treetops in daytime, and the fog
that plagues most of the forest is not as present here as it is elsewhere. Thick
roots cover the ground, almost as in an attempt to trap the inattentive
traveller. The forest continues in all directions.
name: A Dimly Lit Forest

desc:
Large trees sprout up all around, but in the distant northeast and northwest,
the vast prairies spring to life and spread like wildfire to the north. A thin
branch of the forest continues northward from here, and the forest darkens to
the south. A thin mist filters in from the south, and the air around here is
heavy and stagnant.
name: Near the Edge of the Forest

desc:
The forest border is surprisingly dark, especially considering that the prairies
are visible to the north. A light mist covers the ground, but the tree roots
that may pose a problem are not hidden completely. Large oak trees cover the
ground here, and no younger trees seem to be growing anywhere about. The forest
continues to the west and south.
name: Border of the Forest

desc:
Standing here on the borders of the forest, with vast plains to the north, is a
small niche in the forest which is completely isolated from the rest of the
forest, and the only exit being back towards the prairies. Several types of
trees are dominant here, including the regal white birch tree and the massive
oak tree that seems to hold majority on the northern end of the forest.
name: The Forest Borders

desc:
The forest here remains thin as it borders the vast prairies to the north.
However, it seems that the ground cover has grown up to cover what the trees did
not. Several bushes with edible berries grow nearby. To the south the forest
quickly darkens as it progresses closer towards the heart of the forest.
Strangely, there are very few sounds of wildlife anywhere around.
name: The Forest Borders

desc:
The forest here seems to close around the unsuspecting traveller. In fact, it
has. There seems to be no exit from this section of the forest. The trees are
packed tightly together, and any gaps are covered with thick foliage and
undergrowth.
name: Corridor of Trees

desc:
The grass here is burnt to a crisp and the soil coloured a deep black from the
scorching of the earth. A small ditch has been dug a fair distance to the east
of the wall that marks the beginning of the forest, and the grass grows thick
and tall beyond that. The burnt earth continues to the south and north.
name: Scorched Grass

desc:
Golden grass covers the landscape here, giving the area a glowing golden colour
that permeates everything nearby. Just to the south, the green borders of the
Fangorn Forest stand in defiance to the grass. To the west, a strip of land is
burnt black, with a wall of trees visible beyond it.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
The borders of the forest lie just to the south. The magnificent colours of the
forest seem to draw the traveller towards them. Rich and deep green in colour,
they are a perfect contrast to the golden grass that grows on the outskirts
here. In all other directions, but to the south, the golden grass rules the
landscape. Somewhere, far to the north, the faint sound of running water can be
heard.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
The flat landscape stretches out, covered with waist-high bushes and plants,
clinging together in small patches. Long and verdant grass sways in the passing
breezes, and a few trees bow over the plants, their branches hanging like
knotted fingers of giant hands. Towards the east, bushes seem to grow closer
together, almost creating a hedge beside the passing trail.
name: Plains near a Bushy Trail

desc:
The landscape spreads out in a grand vista of long grass and small bushes. Tiny
plants spring up around the thick and tall blades of grass, spiralling upwards
and protected in their growth. Despite the healthy plant life, the ground seems
pebbled and rocky.
name: Waves of Grass

desc:
The land here seems rather barren. Only a little bit of grass grows on it.
Shrubs and some small bushes fill most of these plains, springing up from
between various ploughed patches of earth. Most of the ground lacks plant
growth, although the soil has a deep brown colour. The slightly barren landscape
continues southwards. To the west, the lush greenery indicates another change in
soil and surface.
name: Grassy Shrubs

desc:
Numerous flowers grow scattered on these lush plains. The shrubbery that
dominates to the north fades within the thriving grass here. The greenery is
soft and most likely cushions each step taken on it. Many insects fly around
feeding on the nectar of flowers. Tiny bugs scurry around the stems of the
undergrowth.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
The land is flat. Healthy shrubbery covers the plain. Frequent patches of
flowers provide a rich contrast to the dark green of the bushes. Hills can be
seen far to the west, while more plains are located in all other directions. A
few large bushes that grow here decorate the plain with a random pattern. A
glimpse of village walls can be caught to the south.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
The hills are covered with dense and vibrant foliage. Everything from
berry-bushes to small lily flowers grows here. Small animal trails criss-cross
between grass and plants. Some of the hills barely rise above the plains, while
others are higher, forming steep foothills.
name: Lush Hills

desc:
The land seems to wave up and down here. A tall kind of green grass covers the
hills. To the west, the land slopes higher and higher up towards the mountains.
Small bushes are at the top of every hill, and tiny flowers dot what looks like
a grassy carpet. To the north, the landscape changes, becoming a rocky, grey
surface of stone.
name: Grassy Foothills

desc:
The mountain continues its descent eastwards. To the north, the landscape
steeply descends downwards to a narrow pass far below. It appears as if this
pass might be reached with a great climbing skill. The ground consists of cold,
hard stone, covered with rolling pebbles and flint underfoot. To the west, the
massive rock rises up all the way to the highest peak.
name: Mountain

desc:
Long blades of bright green grass cover the fields. Its thickness and height
make passing these plains quite strenuous. A few trees grow parted from each
other, their branches hanging low. Travelling to the north is impossible, as the
mountainside is steep and offers no footing even for the most skilled climbers.
However, to the west, rocky hills rise, leading upwards on the stony surface.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
This field is full of plants, flowers and bushes of many varieties. The grass
grows in healthy abundance. A few trees grow skywards, scattered across the
field. Their bark has become coarse because of the harsh winds. The plants that
peek through the thick grass offer little insects and small animals something to
feed upon.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
Knee-high grass covers the terrain this area. Small bushes frequently rise up in
scattered patches, varying in type and colour. A few trees are interspersed
within the grass. To the south-east, there grows a small forest with its
treetops reaching high to the sky. The land begins to slope upwards to the
north, leading to the grass-covered foothills at the base of the nearby
mountains.
name: Knee-high Grass

desc:
The trees grow far from each other, varying in size and age. The ground is
covered with a layer of dead leaves, which often cushion the steps of passing
travellers and animals. Some patches of thorny brush invade the forest floor
between the trees. To the east, the woodlands seem to become thicker.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The trees are closely packed together, leaving barely two feet between them.
Some are even closer together, and it appears as if they had grown into each
other. Thick brush consisting of thorns and bushes covers the ground to the
height of an average man's chest. To the south, the thick woodland continues,
while to the east, passage is impossible because village walls prevent everybody
from travelling there.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The trees grow close to each other here in the middle of the forest. The ground
is covered with a tangle of twisty roots and weeds, mixed with prickly plants
that stick to the feet of passing travellers and their mounts, making their
progress slow. To the west, the forest floor seems to be less populated with
undergrowth, and to the south, the land gives way to thorny plains.
name: Heart of the Woodlands

desc:
This wooden gate provides access to a small village behind it. A footpath can be
seen leading from the gate towards the forest to the south. Walls surround the
other sides of the village, closing it off from the forest and plains. The gate
was once sturdy but hangs lopsided on its hinges now.
name: Before a Wooden Gate

desc:
The grass is a thick mat of green blades. The shortest of the blades are about
knee-high to an elf. Some small plants peek through the densely growing grass. A
group of large trees stands in the middle of the field. They rise up in defiance
of everything around them. Looking to the west, a wooden gate leading into a
village can be seen.
name: Dense Fields

desc:
The grass grows thin and patchy. The dark brown soil is the most prevalent
sight. Loose dirt and dust cover the surface and leave little room for plants to
thrive. To the west and the south, the surface seems to change, offering more
nutrients and allowing a variety of plant life. A single tree looms on the
fields to the south.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Thinning rows of grass cover the hard layer of packed dirt beneath. Small plants
and shrubs dominate the other vegetation. A few bushes grow between a variety of
thin and wiry trees. A few signs of animal life can be easily spotted, but even
the buzz of an insect is sometimes heard. To the north, the grasses seem to
thrive and prosper.
name: Dirt-packed Plains

desc:
The grass is covered with a thin layer of brown dust, making it seem burnt and
withered. Some patches of grass have died or faded out, leaving bare areas of
earth visible to the eye. A few prickled and spiny bushes grow on these fields,
unable to shake off the barren look of these lands.
name: Dusty Plains

desc:
Rocky pebbles cover the dirt-packed land. A few smaller weeds have managed to
take root here despite the harsh conditions imposed on them by the dirt.
Boulders are strewn across the grass tufts that are yellowish and withered, as
they have no nutrition to feed on. The low and open plains seem to continue in
all directions.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Despite the barren-looking ground that lies to the east, the grassland thrives
in full force. The soil is moist and enriched by the growth of numerous plants
and flowers. Upon closer examination, a small mound of protruding rocks can be
seen coming out of the grass. To the south, a stand of willows sways its
branches in the wind.
name: Hidden Spring in the Grass

desc:
The plains are covered with a thick carpet of healthy green grass. The long and
wide plains reach out in all directions. Some small bushes grow between the
patches of grass, surrounded by small weeds that feed upon their roots. To the
east, the ground looks moister, indicating the existence of a water source close
by.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Many bushes and plants cover these flat plains. A large and gnarled old tree
stands in the middle of the field. Beside and between its roots, grows various
small plants and moss that feed on the fertile soil. The grass is a thick coat
of long green blades that cover the fields with what seems to be a soft cushion
of green.
name: Brush-filled Fields

desc:
Large and stately trees grow in this forest. Their branches reach high towards
the sky. Countless leaves rustle in the wind. A small, barely visible trail runs
along its course from the north. To the west, the forested area continues,
leading to foothills and a mountain top that towers afar.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The land is lush and teeming with plant life. The grass is coloured in a bright
shade of green and grows long and thick. Large bushes sprout among the grass,
and their branches are filled with leaves. Flowers also peek through the thick
coat of grass and bring a colourful change to the carpet of greenery.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
Many differently coloured flowers appear on the dense layer of verdant grass.
Small plants also thrive here. They are of many different kinds and cluster
around the blooming flowers. Some bushes are set intermittently throughout the
plains. The lush greenery provides good nutrition for many small animals that
are often seen scurrying back and forth, even during night time.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
A few patches of flowers stand in great contrast to the verdant grass that
covers most of the area. The grass is long and thick, and many of the blades
stick straight up into the air. Several bushes grow in small groups scattered
over the field. Some of these bushy places give a home to one or two small
trees. The seemingly endless green fields continue in all directions. However,
to the east, the population of bushes seems to grow denser.
name: Lush Plains

desc:
Lush golden grass covers the land as far as can be seen with the exception of
the foothills to the west and the great Misty Mountains far beyond. To the
distant southeast, the faint sound of a spring can be heard through the silence.
name: Lush Prairie

desc:
Rolling foothills cover the landscape, and the grass here seems almost alive as
it sways back and forth in the breeze. To the east and north, the foothills come
to an abrupt end as prairies sprout up and cover the land in golden grass. To
the west and south, the foothills continue.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
Golden grass covers all the land in sight for miles upon endless miles. To the
north, the foothills begin, while to the west, the tall golden grass has risen
onto a small rise in a desperate attempt to get closer to the sky.
name: Golden Grass

desc:
The foothills here are soft on the feet, as the ground feels almost padded. All
around the foothills, prairies stretch out as far as the eye can see. In a small
valley to the west, a grove of trees battles for the soil that the grass seems
to want to hoard for itself.
name: Foothills of the Misty Mountains

desc:
Vast lands of endless golden grass stretch out in all directions. With the
exception of the Misty Mountains that dominate the western horizon and the dark
forest to the south, the grass covers everything within sight. A low rustling
sound can be heard throughout these lands, as the wind constantly brushes the
tall grasses together.
name: Open Prairie

desc:
Like a great arm, this section of forest splits away from the main body that
lies to the south. Great trees of all varieties grow all around. However, there
seems to be no sign of animal life anywhere around. Not even the chirping of
birds can be heard.
name: An Arm of the Forest

desc:
Outstretched like an arm, this part of the forest is still well lit by the sun
during the day, and faintly by the moon at night. To the south, the forest
becomes darker. But to the north, the woods become a little more sparse as the
forest gives way to the plains that surround its northern borders. To the east,
and to the west, the prairies stretch out. A large black object can be seen to
the north west, breaking through the golden waves of the grasses.
name: An Arm of the Forest

desc:
Amidst the prairies and near the forest lies a small grove of trees. Some
wildlife can be seen here, which one would have thought unusual this close to
the dark forest that lies to the south and west. The tall peaks of the mountains
can be seen over the green leaves that block the view to the west.
name: A Grove by the Forest

desc:
The golden grass that surrounds the landscape seems to rush towards the green
foliage of the forest that lies to the south, almost as if trying to drag any
travellers into the dark depth. Not a single sound can be heard. It is as if
even the grass refuses to rustle in the ominous presence of the dark forest to
the south.
name: Prairie near the Forest

desc:
As the forest approaches the prairies, the battle of green against gold
commences. The dense green of the forest foliage and canopy makes a startling
contrast to the vast golden prairies that lie to the north. The mountains are
vaguely visible in the west and they continue their march ever onward through
time.
name: Prairie near the Forest

desc:
The golden grass runs north like an endless sea, as far as the eye can see and
beyond. To the south, one can enter the depth of the forest, yet to the east, an
impenetrable wall of trees blocks any hope of travel in that direction. The
vague shapes of the Misty Mountains can be seen far off to the west.
name: Prairie near the Forest

desc:
The trees here make a narrow alleyway as they approach the forest. Both to the
east and to the west, passage is blocked by large rows of trees, and the gaps
filled in with dense foliage and bushes. The dark depths of the forest are
visible to the south, and the trees seem to be guiding you in that direction.
name: Corridor of Trees

desc:
Barren land is all that is left here. Once either part of Fangorn or the
prairies around it, this portion of the land is scorched and unrecognizable. One
can only wonder what could have caused such damage to the land. The way west is
completely blocked off by blackened trees and slightly burnt foliage.
name: Scorched Grass

desc:
The lush golden grass of the prairies rages here, but ends abruptly in the west
as the land becomes a barren scorched wasteland before the forest. To the south
and east, the borders of a forest are visible, while the ever-present grey mass
on the western horizon marks the Misty Mountains.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
The ground here is slightly damp and the result on the grass is incredible. The
golden stalks are taller here than anywhere else, standing almost seven feet in
height and blocking the view of everything around. Even with the fertile nature
of the place, however, the only sounds seem to come from the north where running
water can be heard.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
The reeds here seem to never be silenced, between the winds forcing the stalks
to bend and slide against each other and the water that flows in from the north
constantly pressing at the bases of the stalks. The great shape of the Misty
Mountains looms on the distant western horizon. To the south, the land is
covered in tall golden grass.
name: Rustling Reeds

desc:
Golden grass encompasses the landscape, yet somehow a glittering pool of water
has formed in the midst of the vast prairies and lies just to the north. A faint
sound of running water can also be heard in that direction, and the ground to
the east seems to be slightly flooded, forming a small marsh.
name: Near a Pool of Water

desc:
The vast prairies seem to swallow everything in sight. The only break in the
monotony is the ever-present form of the Misty Mountains to the distant west,
and a faint green line on the southern horizon, possibly the great Fangorn
Forest. The faint sounds of birds can be heard far to the north.
name: Vast Prairie

desc:
Miles of lush golden grass cover the lands in all directions. The wind gently
blows at the grass, causing a great rustling sound as the long grass brushes
against its neighbour. On the distant horizon to the west, the southern peaks of
the Misty Mountains are faintly visible, and to the south, a green line above
the fields marks the beginning of a forest in the distance.
name: Windy Prairie

desc:
The green of the forest to the south draws your attention away from the vast
prairies that continue in all other directions. Even the shapes of the great
mountains to the distant west are not as forbidding as the forest that lies at
the southern end of the prairies.
name: Prairie near the Forest

desc:
As the wind blows gently upon the tall stalks of golden grass that grows all
around, the results are only a faint rustling of the stalks sliding against each
other. The vague sound of a cricket breaks the monotony as the sound drifts in
from the north. The forest of Fangorn rises above the grass and draws the eye
southwards.
name: Windy Prairie

desc:
Beautiful golden grass sprouts up and spreads like wildfire in all directions.
Nothing else seems to grow here. Despite the wildlife, the grass shows little
sign of grazing. In the distant west, the southern marches of the Misties
dominate the horizon and to the south, a thin green line is visible between the
golden grass and the sky.
name: Golden Grass

desc:
Miles of golden grass encompass the land in all directions. In the distant west,
the Misty Mountains rise out of the sea of gold and reach far into the sky. A
fair distance to the south, the great Fangorn Forest can be seen as a line of
green between the grass and the sky.
name: Sea of Golden Grass

desc:
Tall grass grows all around, stretching as far as the eye can see. The only
sounds that can be heard are the rustling of the grass as it sways beneath its
own weight and the might of the wind. The massive grey shape of the Misty
Mountains can be seen in the distant west.
name: Endless Prairie

desc:
Long and golden grass flaps in the wind, making a gentle rustling sound. For
miles all around, the golden grass is the only feature that this land has to
offer to the lonely traveller. To the west, the ground starts to roll as the
foothills of the mountains start, while the entire western horizon is covered by
the southern marches of the Misty Mountains.
name: Windy Prairie

desc:
The tall golden stalks seem to be healthier here, a sure sign of nearby water. A
small pond is visible just to the east, and the sound of a river is audible
beyond that. The air is still as the wind is fended off by tall grass.
name: Tall Stalks of Grass

desc:
A small pool of clean water has formed here from the river to the north and
east. To the south and west, a traveller can see the vast plains that this area
is famous for as they seem to stretch on forever from this vantage point. To the
distant west, on the far off horizon, the Mountains stand against the test of
time.
name: A Small Pond in the Prairie

desc:
The river may be shallow, but there is a fair amount of pull to it as it rushes
off to meet the great river Anduin to the distant southeast. Birds can be heard
chirping from unknown locations. The river continues to the north and east. To
the west, the river seems to have formed a small pond, while to the south, the
ground has flooded and mild marshes are visible.
name: A Shallow River

desc:
The river continues its trek from west to south as nature has devised it. The
land to the north climbs up from the river bank into some foothills. To the
east, the river bank is far too high to climb, and is edged by bushes and trees
that seem to make a wall.
name: A Shallow River

desc:
This shallow river seems to house the only significant amount of wildlife in the
area. The river flows quickly to the southeast, but is too shallow to pull
anything of size along. A small pond builds up to the south from a small stream
that branches off from this one, and the mountains loom over the distant horizon
to the west.
name: A Shallow River

desc:
Golden grass grows all around, yet there is no sign of life anywhere. The faint
sound of water comes from somewhere to the north, while to the east a river
flows on its eternal course, promising the possibility of some fish.
name: Quiet Prairie

desc:
Stretching as far as the eye can see, tall golden grass covers these lands and
gives a rich colour to all around it. In the distant east, the faint sound of a
river flows out over the grass while the Misty Mountains cover the horizon to
the west.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
From east to west, north to south, the prairies stretch out, covering the entire
landscape in lush golden grass that stands almost six feet in height at some
points. On the distant western horizon, the Misty Mountains loom over the
prairies, giving hope that the prairies do end somewhere.
name: Endless Prairie

desc:
As far as the eye can see, the entire landscape is covered with lush, golden
grass. Nothing seems to break the monotony around here, just endless grass
stretching from east to west, where the prairies finally reach the distant Misty
Mountains.
name: Monotonous Prairie

desc:
All that can be seen, as far as any mortal eye can see, is endless golden grass.
Yet there is no wildlife anywhere around to be seen. On the distant western
horizon, the great Misty Mountains take to the sky.
name: Endless Grass

desc:
Lush golden grass radiates in all directions, stretching as far as the eye can
see and beyond. To the distant west, past the foothills, the Misty Mountains
rise in defiance of the sky.
name: Lush Prairie

desc:
Everywhere within sight, the prairie continues in an endless wave of golden
grass. To the north, a small pond breaks the monotony of the grass, inviting
wild animals and weary travellers alike to take a drink. The Misty Mountains
loom ominously in the west.
name: Endless Prairie

desc:
Golden grass blankets the landscape and continues on almost endlessly in all
directions, with the exception of the hills to the west. To the east, a pond of
glimmering spring water can be spotted. Far into the western horizon, the Misty
Mountains continue in their endless battle for control over the sky.
name: Windy Prairie

desc:
This small pond seems to be fed from an underwater source, with water as clear
as the best cut diamond. Despite this, little wildlife can be seen anywhere
around here, with the faint chirping of birds being the only sign of life.
name: A Small Pond in the Prairie

desc:
Lush golden grass extends for miles all around. Aside from the occasional small
mammal, the only sound that may be heard is the rustling of the tall grasses as
the winds unceasingly toss them against each other. A small pond to the
southeast breaks the sea of grass and brings a welcome resting spot to any weary
and thirsty traveller in these lands.
name: Quiet Prairie

desc:
For what seems like endless miles, the prairies continue in all directions,
north, east, south and west, as far as the eye can see. The tall grass to the
south blocks the view in that direction, yet the faint sound of a spring can be
vaguely distinguished. On the distant western horizon, the great Misty Mountains
cover the sky.
name: Vast Prairie

desc:
Spiny bushes populate this area of thorny brush, leaving little room to grow for
the small and seemingly weak grass in this area. The thorny shrubs easily mark
the armour and clothes of passing travellers, making travelling here often slow
and tiresome. A single, sick and stunted tree grows amidst the thorny fields.
Its leaves are dead and fell down a long time ago.
name: Thorny Plains

desc:
Waist-high bushes and spiny shrubbery fill these fields for miles. The plants
grow in small groups, leaving little room for grass or flowers. Not many signs
of animal life can be spotted, as these fields have nothing nutritious to offer.
The bushes continue towards all directions but to the south, where a
grass-filled prairie seems to dominate the landscape.
name: Waist-high Brush

desc:
Thorny plants thrive in these fields. They choke almost everything that attempts
to grow here. A few small plants have managed to push themselves through the
thorny brush. Also some weeds have barely survived amidst the multitude of the
prickly vegetation covering the ground. To the west, the landscape changes, as
seemingly better soil offers a good environment for lush grass.
name: Brush-filled Fields

desc:
Small bushes are abundant in the landscape around this place. They randomly grow
in clumps and patches, while others stand alone. Brush and thorns fill the space
between the bushes. Beneath all the foliage, a layer of grass grows green and
healthily.
name: Dense Fields

desc:
The forest is very thin, with a fair amount of distance between the trees. The
ground is covered with sun-dried shrubbery. Thorny plants are abundant, filling
up empty spaces between trees. A layer of dead leaves covers the ground, most of
them in a fair state of decay. The woods continue towards the north. In all
other directions, shrubbery dominates the landscape.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The plains are covered with a dense layer of grass and small plants. Thorns
dominate the flora as they grow up and spread over large parts of the fields,
overgrowing the rest of the undergrowth. Bushes prosper cramped together in
small clumps. To the west, a slightly forested incline rises upwards.
name: Thorny Plains

desc:
Shrubs cover the grass all across the landscape. The grass is bright green and
surrounds the shrubs with a carpet of long blades. Thorny plants also grow
between the various patches of shrubbery. A few trees prosper, scattered among
the shrubs.
name: Shrub-filled Plains

desc:
Flowers and plants sprout up from the fertile fields. The grass is strong and
provides a bright, green background for the vibrant colours of flowers and other
undergrowth. Some bushes grow in large clumps amid the healthy grass. A few
smaller trees grow up between the clumps of other vegetation.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
A large and old tree stands alone in the grass. Its bark is beaten and coarse
because of the weather, a testimony of passing winds and time. The branches are
crooked and hang low. Its leaves are thin and coloured in a pale shade of green.
Most of them that fell on the ground in a circle around the tree have begun to
decay. In contrast to the aged tree, the grass seems to be thriving, reaching
knee-height at most places. To the south, the greenery is a mix of various
colours as a flower-dotted field comes into view.
name: A Tree in the Grass

desc:
A stand of aged willows grows in the middle of this deep green field. The trees
bow down towards the grass, and their branches hang low, as if about to embrace
the earth. Various flowers bloom and sprout up from the grass, topping the green
plains with most of the colours of a rainbow. Small animals scurry around the
stems of the plants trying to remain unseen.
name: A Stand of Willows

desc:
Burnt rings of grass fill these rock-pebbled fields. The ground is cracked and
small crevices run on it like spider webs. Thorny bushes grow throughout the
land, encroaching on the last remains of the grass. A few boulders lie scattered
among the grass, and a bigger pile has formed between a patch of brush. It is
covered with a layer of dust and pebbles, small weedy plants and bits of moss
growing on it.
name: Burnt Grass and Boulders

desc:
Long thin grass grows on the pebbled ground, diminished by the poor soil and
unable to reach its full thickness and height. The ground is dry and rocky, with
only a thin layer of nutritious earth. Small pebbles roll under the footsteps of
travellers. Some green-grey weeds feed on the barren soil. Dust and barren rocks
seem to dominate the surrounding lands.
name: Pebbled Grounds

desc:
Small rocks fill the soil, making it almost inhabitable for any plant life. Some
weeds have managed to plant their roots here, although they appear to be thin
and sick. A couple of shrubs grow in the pebble-packed dirt, but their state
indicates a lack of good nutrition.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The height of a low hill here, although small compared to many other hills,
provides a decent view of the surroundings. At the very top, the miles of golden
grass surrounding the hill is breathtaking. Anybody with a mind for farming
would be drawn to settle this beautiful landscape and live out the rest of their
life tilling the fresh, untained soil. The foothills to the south, and east,
would be perfect for livestock, and the endless plains to the west would produce
an abundance of crops. The hulking shape of the Misty Mountains can be made out
far to the west.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
The foothills are almost nonexistent here. The grass here is a mixture of green
and gold, but in some spots it does not seem to grow for some reason. The
foothills continue to the east and south while the golden grass continues to the
north, and the green grass spreads like wildfire to the west. The Misty
Mountains spread wide on the horizon to the far west.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The foothills here run high, and there is a decent view of the river to the
south as it flows down towards the great river to the distant southeast. A lake
lies close to the west, while foothills rise out in all other directions. These
foothills must have at one time been as tall as the mountains that can be barely
made out in the distant west.
name: The Height of the Plains

desc:
The lake seems to be rather deep, and fed from some underwater source that
cannot be seen. However, the source of the water appears to be located at the
western end of the lake. The water picks up speed as you approach the southern
end of the lake, just before it forms a small river and heads southeast. Green
meadows can be seen to the north and to the west, while on the east bank of the
river a short climb will bring you into some foothills.
name: A Small Lake

desc:
The green grass that springs up all around seems to swallow everything for
miles. The ground here seems to be moist, if not even a little wet. To the
south, a lake seems to shine in the light. To the east, the land starts to roll
as it turns into some minor hills, while to the west and north, the fields turn
a golden colour. Beyond the vast fields and to the distant west, the peaks of
the great Misty Mountains can be seen.
name: Quiet Meadows

desc:
The smooth and even terrain here, with its rich soil, is perfect for growing
crops. Yet there are none in sight, nor even a hint of civilized life anywhere
around. To the east, the land continues until it eventually starts to turn into
gentle foothills. To the south, the grass becomes greener, an evident sign of a
source of fresh water in that direction.
name: Golden Prairie

desc:
In contrast to the lush fields to the west, this area is only covered with
sparse and thin patches of grass. At many places, the ground is bare. At others,
thin and wiry stems of grass have sprouted with a yellowish-green shade, which
indicates a lack of nutrients in the soil. Dirt and pebbles seem to cover the
lands more to the north and the east.
name: Sparse Plains

desc:
The barren plains that dominate the eastern fields seem to give way to fresh
earth here. The soil is richer and healthier, offering grass a new chance to
grow. Deep green tufts sprout from the surface. Small weeds and flowers poke
their blooms and leaves up towards the sky.
name: Earthen Plains

desc:
Numerous blooms dominate this part of the grassland, reaching out in an array of
countless colours. Growing together in clumps, they form patches of yellow,
white and blue, breaking up the monotony of the green carpet of grass. The soft
and moist soil has also welcomed a few larger bushes that thrive here, growing
between the flowering plants.
name: Enriched Fields

desc:
The surrounding fields are covered with grass that grows about two feet high,
untamed and hardy. Several blooms of white and blue flowers spring up out of the
grass, reaching for the sun. Insects buzz around the flowers feeding on the
results of the pollen. The lush greenery continues in all directions.
name: Fertile Plains

desc:
All around for miles, the prairies continue almost endlessly. However, far to
the east, gentle rolling hills can barely be discerned. The vegetation turns
greener further to the south, possibly nurtured by fresh water.
name: Quiet Prairie

desc:
The golden grass that springs up all around seems to continue endlessly to the
north and to the west. To the east and south, the gold turns to green as the
lands approach the river that can be heard far off to the southeast. Far off to
the west, the great Misty Mountains rise above the horizon.
name: Quiet Prairie

desc:
This beautiful meadow seems to house a variety of small animals and insects. The
lake to the east is almost blocked off from this meadow by some bushes and
shrubs, while everywhere else golden grass springs up into the horizon.
name: Meadow by the Lake

desc:
The grass here is a mixture of golden prairie grass and the green grass of a
meadow. To the east, the green grass dominates as it comes closer to a lake in
the distant east. Everywhere else around you, the golden grass is dominant and
spreads as far as the eye can see.
name: Lush Prairie

desc:
Golden grass covers the countryside for miles and more seemingly endless miles.
Tall grass blocks the view of everything around. Yet always present in the
distant west, the Misty Mountains are barely visible over the grass.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Endless prairies continue in all directions, covering the ground, and in the odd
case, the grass seems to be attempting to reach the sky. To the west, a
glimmering pond can be seen, and past that, on the distant horizon, mountains
loom ominously.
name: Lush Tall Grass

desc:
Golden grass stretches as far as the eye can see in all directions. However, all
things must come to an end, and in the distant west, the Misty Mountains stand,
putting an end to the seemingly limitless prairies that lie all around.
name: Endless Prairie

desc:
Grass, tall, lively and golden, is everywhere. For many miles, this grass is all
that there is to be seen. Above the grass, and to the distant west, the shapes
of the southern peaks of the Misty Mountains dominate the sky.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Soft tall grass sprouts up all around. Endless in all directions, except to the
north where a dense patch of shrubs grow, the prairies seem to continue on
forever. In the distant west, the southern marches of the Misty Mountains can be
seen.
name: The Northern End of the Prairie

desc:
Thick and spiny bushes densely overgrow these fields, dominating the few trees
that manage to push upwards through the thick and thorny undergrowth. In small
areas where the bushes have not managed to complete the cover, the grass tufts
fight for nutrition and light against the dense shrubbery.
name: Dense Shrubs

desc:
Foliage and plant life is abundant in these fields. Despite the denser shrubs
that seem to take hold to the west, the grass grows knee-high here, allowing
flowers to bloom and sprout up between its healthy blades. Small insects buzz
around, collecting nectar for their nurture, and the signs of several other
animals can be spotted. The fertile grasslands continue to the north and the
east.
name: Lush Fields

desc:
Various clumps of bushes thrive in the fertile soil, accompanied by lush and
thick grass, growing to a height of almost two feet. At a few places, the tops
of flowers pop up amidst the tall greenery, giving it a speckled look. The
fields seem to continue towards all directions. The walls of a small village can
be spotted further to the north-west.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
Thorny shrubs and weeds densely grow beside each other, filling nearly every bit
of space amidst the grass. A few smaller trees and bushes grow here along with
other plants in the fertile soil. To the south, the vegetation seems to become
thicker, as the grass disappears from view. A variety of weeds now dominates
what one can see.
name: Bushy Fields

desc:
A rich growth of algae, moss, and liverwort covers the rocks in the roaring,
mist-shrouded falls. As the river flows from the deep pool at the base of the
cascade it quickly picks up speed, funnelled between steep rocky riverbanks. The
river froths and churns as it moves rapidly through the densely wooded
landscape.
name: Rapids below the Falls

desc:
The water here is a maelstrom of sound and crushing power as masses of water
flow down from the river far above. The river runs wildly here, swirling and
pulling at you, sucking you below the surface and pulling you towards the fast
flowing rapids to the east.
name: Within a Pool of Thunder

desc:
An ancient decaying oak looms overhead, laid to rest across the fast flowing
river. The fallen tree forms a natural bridge across the river, although judging
by the large chunks of decaying wood that occasionally fall from its bulk, It
may prove to be unfriendly to those who attempt its passage.
name: Beneath a Fallen Tree

desc:
Fierce currents from the pressure of the pounding water crashing down the
waterfall from the river above, suck at the arms and legs, pulling you from side
to side and pushing you down into the plunge pools depths. The powerful down
force of the crashing churning water tires the body and saps the will to fight
for the faint light so near yet so far above.
name: Within the Mists
